component.name: Tabs
component.description: Organizes related content into selectable panels on a single page, so only one panel shows at a time.
component.note: tablist of Tab buttons over a 1px track + tabpanels. Selected: forest.500 text + 3px forest underline; unselected neutral.500 (hover neutral.600). Inter medium 14/20. Roving-tabindex arrow-key nav with automatic activation. Synced to Figma '❖ Tabs'.

useCases: Split related content into sections on one page (Overview / Activity / Settings)
Group settings or detail panels under one header

commonPatterns.name: Basic tabs
commonPatterns.description: Provide tabs with labels and content; the first enabled tab is selected by default.

commonPatterns.name: Controlled
commonPatterns.description: Own the selected id to sync with the URL or external state.

commonPatterns.name: With a disabled tab
commonPatterns.description: Disabled tabs are skipped by keyboard navigation.

antiPatterns.reason: Tabs imply independent, freely-switchable sections, not an ordered flow.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a stepper / wizard with a Progress bar.

antiPatterns.scenario: Tabs for navigating between separate pages/URLs as primary nav
antiPatterns.reason: Tabs organize content on one page; cross-page nav belongs elsewhere.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use the app's primary navigation (Menu / Page Layout).

antiPatterns.scenario: Too many tabs to fit on one line
antiPatterns.reason: Overflowing tabs are hard to scan and reach.
antiPatterns.alternative: Reduce sections, or use a Select / Menu for overflow.

antiPatterns.scenario: Hiding critical content behind a non-default tab
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep essential content on the default tab or outside tabs.

slots.tabs: Required — array of { id?, label, content, isDisabled? }

interactions.select: Click or arrow-to a tab to show its panel
interactions.keyboard: Arrow Left/Right/Up/Down move and activate (automatic); Home/End jump to first/last; disabled tabs skipped
interactions.focus: 2px forest.700 inset outline on the focused tab

behavior.controlled: Controlled via selectedId + onChange; uncontrolled via defaultSelectedId (defaults to first enabled).

accessibility.wiring: Each tab aria-controls its panel; each panel aria-labelledby its tab; selected tab aria-selected=true
accessibility.rovingTabindex: Only the selected tab is in the tab order (tabIndex 0); the rest are -1 and reached via arrows
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Tab to enter the tablist, Arrows to switch, Tab again to reach the panel
accessibility.screenReader: Announced as a tab list with selected state

athenaConstraints.indicator: 3px forest.500 underline on the selected tab

figmaDivergences.reason: Athena's system font is Inter; Atlassian Sans was the Figma kit default.

aiHints.context: Reach for Tabs to split related content into switchable panels on one page. Provide a declarative tabs array. Use for peer sections, not ordered steps or cross-page navigation. Keep the count small enough to fit one line.
neverUseWhen: The content is an ordered sequence — use a stepper/wizard
It's primary cross-page navigation — use the app nav
There are too many sections to fit — reduce or use a Select
Essential content would hide behind a secondary tab — surface it by default
